title: 10 best herbal creams for period cramps

remedy_properties: Thymol: Thymol has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation associated with menstrual cramps by blocking the production of prostaglandins that cause uterine contractions.

Carnosol: Carnosol is a powerful antioxidant that has anti-inflammatory and anti-pyretic effects, which helps to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort caused by period cramps by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in wormwood have antispasmodic properties, which help to relax smooth muscle tissue in the uterus, reducing uterine contractions and alleviating menstrual cramp symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Artemisia absinthium, also known as wormwood, creams helps with period cramps because of its potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The active compounds present in wormwood, such as absinthin and thujone, have been traditionally used to relieve menstrual pain and discomfort. These compounds work by reducing inflammation in the uterine muscles and blocking the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause pain and cramping. As a result, wormwood creams can provide natural relief from period cramps, allowing women to manage their symptoms more effectively.
remedy_constituents: Thujone: Thujone has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate period cramps by reducing inflammation and relaxing uterine muscles.
Caffeic acid: Caffeic acid is a phenolic compound with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with menstrual cramps.

Artemisinin: Artemisinin is a sesquiterpene lactone with anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help reduce inflammation and relax uterine muscles, leading to relief from period cramps.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in thujone, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate cramp pain.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and terpenoids that contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, providing relief from menstrual cramps.
Stems: Similar to leaves, stems contain thujone, which may help to relax muscles and reduce cramp severity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried artemisia absinthium flowers and leaves from a clean area with a sterile knife.
Infuse 2 cups of oil such as sweet almond oil with the dried herb in a double boiler for 4 hours.
Strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container discarding the solids.
Mix 1 tablespoon of beeswax with 2 tablespoons of the infused oil in a double boiler until the beeswax melts.
Pour the cooled mixture into a clean container and let it set at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Sage contains compounds that help relax uterine muscles, reducing spasms and cramping associated with menstrual periods. This property helps alleviate pain and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help reduce inflammation in the pelvic area, which can contribute to period cramps, bloating, and other symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate menstrual pain and discomfort, promoting overall uterine health.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, creams helps with period cramps because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The cream's active compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid, work together to reduce inflammation and block pain signals to the brain. This results in a decrease in pain and discomfort associated with menstrual cramps. Additionally, sage's antispasmodic properties help to relax the uterine muscles, further alleviating cramping and promoting a sense of relief and comfort.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce pain and discomfort associated with menstrual cramps.
Salvianolic acid B: This polyphenol has potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate inflammation and pain in the uterus during menstrual cramps.

Ursolic acid: This triterpenoid has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with menstrual cramps, as well as improve overall menstrual health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in salvene and rosmarinic acid, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Roots: They contain salvianolic acid, which is known for its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25g of salvia officinalis leaves and flowers at the peak of their potency and dry them thoroughly in a warm place for 2 weeks.
Combine the dried salvia officinalis with 100g of beeswax and 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 20 minutes while stirring occasionally until the beeswax and coconut oil are fully incorporated.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and let it cool for 10 minutes before adding 10g of vitamin e oil and 5g of aloe vera gel.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool completely at room temperature for 30 minutes before use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate period pain by blocking prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions.

Pain-relieving properties: Herbal ginger creams contain natural analgesics like gingerol, which has been shown to have a pain-relieving effect on the body, helping to reduce period cramp severity and duration.

Vasodilatory effects: Ginger creams may help to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, which can help to reduce menstrual cramps by increasing oxygen and nutrient delivery to the uterus and reducing constriction of uterine blood vessels.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with period cramps because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, work to reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause pain and discomfort during menstruation. By applying ginger cream to the lower abdomen, it can help relax the uterine muscles and alleviate cramps, allowing women to find relief from the discomfort associated with period cramps. This natural remedy can provide a much-needed respite during this time.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These sesquiterpenoid compounds are responsible for ginger's anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce prostaglandins and alleviate cramp pain associated with menstruation.
Shogaols: These sesquiterpenoid compounds are also present in ginger, and they have been found to have potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help relieve menstrual cramp pain.

Curcumin: A phenolic compound found in ginger, curcumin has been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which can help reduce menstrual cramp pain and alleviate associated symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Extracts from the rhyzomes contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate period cramps.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are used to make creams that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with menstrual cramps due to their high gingerol content.
Buds: Ginger buds are used to create creams that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to ease menstrual cramp pain.
remedy_recipe: Infuse the dried roots in 2 cups of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to create a strong herbal decoction.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl.
Combine 1 cup of shea butter and 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add 2 tablespoons of the herbal decoction to the shea butter and coconut oil mixture and stir well.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and becomes a smooth, creamy texture taking about 10 minutes.

remedy_properties: Passionflower (Maypop's main ingredient): Passionflower has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which can help reduce pain associated with menstrual cramps.
Fragarine: Fragarine, a compound found in maypop, has been demonstrated to possess antispasmodic properties, which can help relax uterine muscles and alleviate cramping.
Saponins: Maypop creams often contain saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with menstrual pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata, also known as maypop, creams helps with period cramps because of its unique ability to interact with the body's natural pain-relieving mechanisms. The herb contains flavonoids and alkaloids that have a sedative and antispasmodic effect, which can help to ease muscle cramps and spasms associated with menstrual cramps. Additionally, Passiflora incarnata creams may help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, further contributing to its relief of period cramps and associated discomfort. This natural remedy can be a welcome alternative to traditional pain relievers.
remedy_constituents: Harmane: A tryptamine alkaloid, harmane has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with menstrual cramps.

Flavonoids (specifically, kaempferol and isorhapontigenin): These phenolic compounds have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain caused by menstrual cramps.

Isoorientin: A flavonoid glycoside, isoorientin has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with menstrual cramps, and also exhibit a sedative effect to reduce stress and anxiety.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their flavonoid and alkaloid content, which are believed to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Used for their rich content of flavonoids and alkaloids, which are thought to help alleviate period cramps and other symptoms associated with PMS.
Fruits: Used due to their high content of vitamins and minerals, particularly vitamin C, potassium, and magnesium, which can help in relaxing muscles and reducing cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of dried passiflora incarnata flowers in late summer or early fall.
Steep the dried flowers in 100 milliliters of carrier oil like sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Add 5-7 milliliters of beeswax and 5-7 milliliters of candelilla wax to the mixture and heat until melted.
Pour the mixture into small tin containers and let it cool and solidify completely.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Ceylon cinnamon's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde, help reduce inflammation in the uterus and other tissues, alleviating menstrual cramp discomfort.
Pain-relieving: The analgesic and antispasmodic properties of herbal Ceylon cinnamon creams help block pain pathways, reducing the severity of period cramps and spasms.
Antioxidant: Ceylon cinnamon's high antioxidant content helps protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to menstrual discomfort and cramps.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum, also known as Ceylon cinnamon, creams helps with period cramps because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The cinnamon oil present in these creams reduces prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause the uterus to contract, leading to pain and discomfort during menstruation. By applying topical creams, the body absorbs the cinnamon oil, which helps to relax the uterine muscles, providing relief from cramps and other menstrual symptoms. Regular use may also help to reduce menstrual pain over time.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help in reducing pain and inflammation associated with menstrual cramps.
Cinnamic acid: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that contribute to period cramps.
Coumarin: This phenolic compound has a mild analgesic effect and may help in reducing pain perception, thereby providing relief from menstrual cramps.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in essential oils, particularly eugenol, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help alleviate cramps and discomfort.
Barks: Cinnamon bark contains cinnamaldehyde, which has been traditionally used to reduce pain and inflammation associated with menstrual cramps.
Buds: The buds of Cinnamomum verum are rich in essential oils with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them a potential ingredient in creams for period cramp relief.
remedy_recipe: Infuse cinnamomum verum bark in 2 cups of distilled water for 30 minutes at 100 degrees fahrenheit.
Strain the infusion and mix it with 1/2 cup of aloe vera gel and 1/4 cup of beeswax pellets.
Melt the beeswax in a double boiler for 10 minutes while gently stirring the mixture.
Add 2 tablespoons of vitamin e oil and 1 teaspoon of cetyl alcohol to the melted beeswax mixture.
Pour the mixture into glass containers and store them in a cool dark place for 24 hours.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Highbush cranberry creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids and polyphenols, which help to reduce inflammation in the body, alleviating menstrual cramp pain.
Antispasmodic properties: The herbal extracts in highbush cranberry creams have antispasmodic properties that relax uterine muscles, reducing spasms and contractions associated with period cramps.
Vasoconstrictive effects: The compounds present in highbush cranberry creams can help to constrict blood vessels, which may reduce blood flow to the uterus and alleviate cramping and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Vaccinium macrocarpon, also known as highbush cranberry, creams helps with period cramps because it contains anthocyanins and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation and pain in the uterus, thereby alleviating cramps. Additionally, the high antioxidant content in Vaccinium macrocarpon creams may also help to regulate hormonal imbalances that can contribute to period cramps, promoting a more comfortable and manageable menstrual experience. It is often used as an alternative remedy to traditional pain-relievers.
remedy_constituents: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce the severity of period cramps by soothing the uterine muscles and reducing inflammation.

Quercetin: A flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, quercetin may help alleviate period cramps by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions.

Catechins: These polyphenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which may help reduce the severity of period cramps by inhibiting the production of pain-producing chemicals in the body.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their tannin content, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate menstrual cramps.
Fruits: Used due to their anthocyanin content, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce period cramps.
Leaves: Used also due to their flavonoid content, which has antioxidant properties that can help to reduce pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of vaccinium macrocarpon berries and dry them in a dehydrator at 45c for 8 hours.
Combine the dried berries with 200ml of distilled water and 10g of beeswax in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally to create a smooth paste.
Add 20g of vitamin e oil and 10g of shea butter to the paste and mix until well combined.
Transfer the mixture to a double boiler and let it cool and thicken for 2 hours.

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Valerian root has been shown to relax uterine muscles, reducing spasms and cramping associated with menstrual periods.
Anti-inflammatory property: Valerian's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause pain and inflammation during menstruation.

Sedative and anxiolytic properties: Valerian has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate anxiety and stress often associated with menstrual cramps, promoting relaxation and reducing discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian, creams helps with period cramps because of its natural sedative and pain-relieving properties. The cream's active compounds, such as valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, work together to relax the uterine muscles and reduce inflammation, providing fast and effective relief from menstrual cramps. Additionally, valerian creams often contain other soothing ingredients like lavender and chamomile, which promote relaxation and calmness, helping to alleviate period-related discomfort and promote overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Isovaleric acid: This organic acid helps alleviate period cramps by acting as a natural analgesic, reducing pain perception in the body.
Valerenic acid: A sesquiterpene derivative, valerenic acid exhibits sedative and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to relax the uterus, reducing menstrual cramp severity and duration.
Valerenol: A valerenol is a valerenic acid isomer that has been shown to have a potent anxiolytic effect and relax muscles, which helps to alleviate menstrual cramp pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian root is commonly used due to its high concentration of valerenic acids and valeranone, which are believed to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Valerian leaves are sometimes used, although less frequently than roots or rhizomes, due to their lower content of active compounds.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 50g of dried valeriana officinalis root in 500ml of boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh into a clean glass container.
Mix 20g of beeswax with 10g of coconut oil in a double boiler set over low heat.
Combine the infused liquid with the melted wax mixture and stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into glass jars and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chamomile cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like apigenin and luteolin, which help reduce inflammation and relax muscle spasms in the uterus, providing relief from period cramps.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in chamomile cream, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate period cramp symptoms, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.

Muscle relaxants: Chamomile's muscle-relaxing properties, particularly the sedative effects of apigenin, help alleviate cramps by relaxing the uterine muscles and reducing spasms, providing rapid relief from menstrual pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with period cramps because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream contains apigenin, an active compound that binds to GABA receptors in the brain, producing a calming effect and reducing pain perception. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties help to relax the uterine muscles, reducing cramping and discomfort associated with menstruation. This natural remedy provides relief and comfort for women experiencing period cramps, allowing them to manage their symptoms and maintain their daily activities.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which helps alleviate period cramps by reducing prostaglandin levels and modulating pain perception.
α-Bisabolol: A sesquiterpene alcohol with anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which helps relieve period cramps by relaxing uterine muscle contractions and reducing inflammation.

Luteolin: A flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which helps alleviate period cramps by reducing prostaglandin levels, modulating pain perception, and protecting against oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used for their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to ease menstrual cramp pain.
Leaves: Used for their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to relax the uterine muscles and reduce cramping.
Seeds: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and ease menstrual cramp pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried flowers and leaves of matricaria chamomilla on a sunny morning at 8am.
Steam distill the harvested matricaria chamomilla for 2 hours to obtain 50ml of essential oil.
Mix 50g of beeswax with 50g of shea butter and 20ml of sweet almond oil in a double boiler at 180°f.
Add 10ml of the steam-distilled essential oil and 5 drops of vitamin e oil to the melted mixture.
Pour the cooled mixture into a container and store it in a cool dry place for 2 days.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Nettle creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and carotenoids, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain caused by menstrual cramps.

Pain-relieving omega-6 fatty acids: Nettle creams are rich in omega-6 fatty acids, particularly gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), which helps regulate prostaglandins and reduce their constriction of the uterine muscles, alleviating menstrual cramp pain.

Hormone-balancing phytoestrogens: Nettle creams contain phytoestrogens, plant-based compounds that mimic estrogen's effects in the body. These phytoestrogens help balance hormonal fluctuations during menstruation, reducing symptoms like cramps, bloating, and mood swings.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle, creams helps with period cramps because of its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. The cream contains compounds like flavonoids and carotenoids, which work to reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions and lead to cramping. Additionally, stinging nettle has natural relaxant properties, helping to calm the uterine muscles and alleviate menstrual discomfort. As a result, many women find relief from period cramps and discomfort when using Urtica dioica creams.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Urtica dioica creams, particularly quercetin, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce prostaglandin production, which contributes to period cramp pain.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as ferulic acid, have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help alleviate period cramp pain by inhibiting the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body.

Alkaloids (histamine and other minor alkaloids): While the effects of alkaloids in Urtica dioica are less understood, histamine and other minor alkaloids may have a role in reducing inflammation and modulating neurotransmitter activity, which could contribute to pain relief during periods.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Urtica dioica for period cramp relief due to their high content of anti-inflammatory compounds such as histamine and polyphenols.
Roots: Roots are used for their diuretic properties, which help to reduce water retention and alleviate cramping associated with menstrual cycles.
Stems: Stems of Urtica dioica are used due to their rich content of flavonoids and other anti-inflammatory compounds that help to soothe and calm menstrual cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh urtica dioica leaves in the early morning on a dry day.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at 30°c for 2 hours or until crisp.
Combine 50g of dried leaves with 250ml of jojoba oil in a double boiler.
Simmer the mixture at 40°c for 2 hours or until the oil has turned greenish in color.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and bottle the resulting cream in dark glass containers.

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lemon balm's antispasmodic compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and apigenin, help relax uterine muscles, reducing menstrual cramp severity by diminishing spasms and pain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory compounds, like flavonoids and phenolic acids, reduce inflammation in the uterus and surrounding tissues, which can contribute to period cramps.

Anxiolytic (calming) properties: Lemon balm's anxiolytic compounds, such as citral and nerolidol, have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and stress that often accompany menstrual cramps.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, creams helps with period cramps because they contain compounds like rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. These properties help to relax the uterine muscles, reducing spasms and cramping. Additionally, the calming and soothing effects of Melissa officinalis can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate period cramps. By applying a Melissa officinalis cream to the skin, the active ingredients can be absorbed, providing relief from period cramps and promoting overall comfort.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic Acid: As a phenolic compound, rosmarinic acid exhibits anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate period cramps by reducing inflammation and pain in the uterine muscles.

Linalool: This terpene compound has a sedative effect, which can help relieve anxiety and stress associated with menstrual cramps, and also exhibits analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties to provide pain relief.

Volatile Oils (including linalyl acetate and geranial): These terpene compounds in Melissa officinalis have analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with period cramps, as well as promote relaxation and reduce anxiety.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and relieve menstrual cramps.
Flowers: They have a calming effect and are rich in essential oils, which can help to reduce stress and alleviate period cramp symptoms.
Stems: They contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help to ease menstrual cramps and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 20 grams of melissa officinalis flowers in 200 milliliters of cold-pressed oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 to 21 days shaking it daily.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container.
Add 10 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of distilled water to the oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat while stirring constantly until the wax is fully incorporated and the mixture has thickened.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with period cramps because it contains bioactive compounds that work to relax the uterine muscles, reducing menstrual cramp severity. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe and calm the discomfort caused by prostaglandins, hormone-like substances produced during menstruation. Additionally, dandelion's natural diuretic effects can help reduce bloating and water retention, further alleviating period pain symptoms. As a result, using herbal dandelion creams as a topical treatment can provide fast and effective relief from period cramps.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation and ease muscle spasms caused by period cramps, providing relief from discomfort.
Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic compounds in dandelion cream help relax the uterine muscles, reducing cramp severity and frequency, allowing for a more comfortable menstrual experience.

Astringent properties: Dandelion's astringent properties can help reduce menstrual bleeding and alleviate heavy flow issues associated with period cramps, promoting a sense of relief and reduced discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe cramp-related discomfort and reduce inflammation.
Fruits: Contain a natural pain-relieving compound called taraxasterol, which helps alleviate menstrual cramps and spasms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dandelion roots weighing approximately 200 grams.
Dry the dandelion roots in a cool dark place for 7 days or until brittle.
Grind the dried dandelion roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 100 grams of the powdered dandelion root with 50 milliliters of coconut oil and heat.
Let the mixture simmer for 30 minutes then strain and fill glass jars.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn creams helps with period cramps because they contain flavonoids and procyanidins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce pain and discomfort. The natural compounds in hawthorn also improve blood flow to the uterus, reducing inflammation and tension that can contribute to menstrual cramp severity. By relaxing uterine muscles, hawthorn creams can provide relief from cramping, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating period symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Hawthorn's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and pain associated with menstrual cramps, making it easier to manage discomfort.
Vasodilatory effects: Hawthorn's ability to dilate blood vessels can help improve circulation, which may help alleviate cramping caused by reduced blood flow during menstruation.
Antispasmodic properties: Hawthorn's antispasmodic compounds can help relax uterine muscles and reduce spasms that contribute to menstrual cramps, providing relief from discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hawthorn roots have been traditionally used to ease menstrual discomfort due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them a popular choice for soothing period cramp-related pain.
Barks: The barks of the hawthorn tree contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to relax muscle spasms and reduce inflammation, providing relief from period cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh crataegus monogyna leaves and 10g of fresh cirsium eriophorum roots dry them for at least 48 hours.
Combine the dried ingredients in a mortar and pestle grind them into a fine powder using 10 gentle strokes.
Mix the powdered herbal blend with 20ml of sweet almond oil 5% w/w add vitamin e oil as an emulsifier if necessary.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a dedicated herb-making machine at 60°c for 30 minutes to facilitate infusion and blending.
Strain the cream through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into clean glass jars store it in the refrigerator for up to 6 months using within 3 months for optimal potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with period cramps because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. Lavender oil, a key ingredient in these creams, contains linalool, a compound that relaxes muscle spasms and reduces inflammation. When applied topically to the skin, it absorbs into the bloodstream, targeting the uterine lining and relaxing the uterus, providing relief from cramp pain. Additionally, lavender's calming scent can also help reduce stress and anxiety associated with menstrual cramps, promoting a sense of relaxation and comfort.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lavender's antispasmodic properties help to relax muscles and reduce spasms, which can ease period cramp discomfort by reducing muscle contractions in the uterus.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the uterine lining, which can contribute to reduced cramping intensity and frequency.
Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: The calming scent of lavender has a soothing effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and stress levels that can exacerbate period cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in compounds that help to relax muscles and reduce inflammation, making them effective in alleviating cramping discomfort.
Seeds: Containing anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, lavender seeds can be extracted to create a soothing cream that helps ease menstrual cramp pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine the powdered lavender with 20 grams of cold-pressed coconut oil and 10 grams of beeswax in a double boiler on medium heat for 30 minutes.
Stir the mixture frequently as it melts, dissolving the ingredients until a smooth consistency is achieved after 15 minutes of heating.
Remove the double boiler from the heat source and let it cool to around 60°c for 10 minutes before whipping the mixture into a creamy texture using an immersion blender for 5 minutes.
Transfer the cooled cream to sterilized glass containers, label them, and store them in the refrigerator at a temperature of 4°c for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with period cramps because they contain curcumin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that reduces prostaglandins production in the body. These hormonal fluctuations cause uterine contractions leading to severe menstrual pain. Curcumin in turmeric cream binds to prostaglandins receptors, thereby reducing their effects and alleviating cramping symptoms. Additionally, turmeric's natural warming properties help relax muscle spasms, providing instant relief from discomfort. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to soothe menstrual cramps, offering a gentle and effective alternative to over-the-counter pain relievers.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with menstrual cramps.
Cineole: Cineole, a natural terpene found in turmeric, has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that can help ease period pain and discomfort.

Caffeoylquinic acids: These compounds have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and estrogen-regulating properties that may help alleviate menstrual cramps by reducing inflammation and hormone imbalance.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant contain a high concentration of curcuminoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate period cramp pain.

Rhyzomes: Turmeric's rhyzomes are the underground stems that store nutrients and medicinal compounds, making them rich in curcumin and other bioactive compounds that reduce inflammation and relax muscles to relieve menstrual cramps.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 50 grams of unrefined coconut oil and transfer it to a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Grind 10 grams of dried turmeric root (curcuma longa) into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground turmeric powder with 20 grams of beeswax pellets in a small saucepan over low heat stirring constantly for 5 minutes.
Add the heated turmeric-beeswax mixture to the coconut oil and blend until smooth using an immersion blender taking approximately 2 minutes.
Pour the curcuma longa cream into sterilized glass jars filling them to the top and letting it cool completely before capping.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gromwell creams helps with period cramps because they contain a natural ingredient that has been used for centuries to ease menstrual discomfort. The herb, also known as Leontodon hispidus, is rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that work to relax the uterine muscles, reducing cramping and spasms associated with PMS. When applied topically as a cream, gromwell's active ingredients are absorbed quickly into the skin, providing fast relief from period cramps without disrupting daily activities or causing drowsiness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Gromwell contains anti-inflammatory compounds like sesquiterpenes, which help to reduce inflammation and ease menstrual cramp pain by blocking prostaglandins from binding to receptors.
Antispasmodic properties: Gromwell has been shown to have antispasmodic effects, which can help relax uterine muscles and alleviate cramping caused by excessive contraction during menstruation.
Pain-relieving flavonoids: The flavonoid-rich extract of gromwell cream contains pain-relieving compounds that inhibit the production of prostaglandins, thereby reducing menstrual cramp intensity.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, the roots help alleviate menstrual cramp symptoms by reducing inflammation and relaxing muscles.
Rhizomes: The rhizome's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce prostaglandin levels, which contribute to menstrual cramps, making it a key ingredient in period pain relief creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lithospermum officinale flowers in early morning and clean them thoroughly under running water.
Dry the cleaned lithospermum officinale flowers using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 24 hours.
Combine one part dried lithospermum officinale with three parts coconut oil and mix well until a smooth paste forms.
Add 5% vitamin e oil and 2% tea tree essential oil to the mixture and blend thoroughly.
Fill sterilized jars with the cream, seal tightly, and store in the refrigerator for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Masterwort creams helps with period cramps because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and relax the uterine muscles, reducing pain and discomfort. The cream's active ingredients, such as flavonoids and terpenes, also help to reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions and exacerbate menstrual cramp symptoms. By addressing the underlying causes of period cramps, masterwort creams provide effective relief from discomfort, allowing women to manage their symptoms naturally and maintain a sense of well-being during menstruation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Masterwort creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation in the uterus and alleviate pain caused by menstrual cramps.

Antispasmodic properties: The herbal extract in masterwort creams has antispasmodic properties that relax smooth muscle contractions in the uterus, reducing spasms and cramping associated with menstruation.
Pain-relieving compounds: Masterwort creams contain pain-relieving compounds like salicylic acid, which is similar to aspirin, helping to reduce pain and discomfort caused by period cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: Masterwort roots are rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds that help alleviate cramping symptoms by relaxing uterine muscles and reducing inflammation.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of the masterwort plant contain saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce period pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 tablespoons of dried peucedanum ostruthium root and grind it into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Combine the ground peucedanum ostruthium with 6 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat at low temperature for 10 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and stir in 2 teaspoons of beeswax until fully dissolved and melted after 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into small glass jars and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes before use.
Store the finished creams in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerate for up to 1 year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild rose creams helps with period cramps because they contain a blend of natural ingredients that work together to reduce inflammation and soothe the uterine muscles. The rosehip oil in these creams is particularly effective at reducing prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions and lead to cramping. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of wild rose and other herbal extracts help to calm the digestive system and alleviate bloating and discomfort associated with menstruation, providing relief from period cramps and promoting a sense of overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Wild rose contains flavonoids and terpenoids that relax uterine muscles, reducing menstrual cramp severity by soothing spasms and contractions.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The herb's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce prostaglandins, hormones that cause uterus muscle contractions and lead to cramping, alleviating pain and discomfort.
Pain-relieving properties: Wild rose contains salicylic acid, a natural derivative of aspirin, which helps block pain pathways in the brain, providing relief from menstrual cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help alleviate menstrual cramp symptoms.
Seeds: Contain fatty acids and vitamins that soothe digestive issues and reduce bloating associated with period cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried rose petals at dawn when their potency is highest according to traditional folklore.
Combine 20 grams of dried rose petals with 10 grams of calendula flowers and 5 grams of chamomile flowers in a clean glass jar.
Infuse the herbal mixture in 100 milliliters of cold-pressed olive oil for 4 weeks shaking daily to extract optimal essence.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another glass jar discarding solids and retaining clear liquid.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax with 20 milliliters of the herbal-infused oil heating gently until melted then pour into sterilized containers for cooling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with period cramps because they contain compounds that relax the uterine muscles, reducing inflammation and pain. The anti-inflammatory properties of fennel help to ease cramping, while its natural anesthetizing effects provide relief from discomfort. Additionally, fennel's ability to stimulate digestion can help alleviate symptoms of bloating and nausea often associated with menstruation, further enhancing the cream's overall effectiveness in providing relief from period cramps.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Fennel contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, lignans, and terpenes that help reduce inflammation in the uterus and alleviate menstrual cramps.

Estrogen balancer: Fennel has estrogen-balancing properties, which can help regulate hormonal imbalances that contribute to period cramps by reducing prostaglandins' production, a hormone-like substance that causes uterine contractions.

Anxiolytic and antispasmodic effects: The essential oils in fennel, such as anethole, have anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) and antispasmodic properties that can help soothe cramps, reduce anxiety associated with menstruation, and relax uterine muscles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate menstrual cramp pain.
Seeds: Containing anethole, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound that can provide relief from period cramps and associated discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare leaves in the summer season ensuring they are dry and free from contaminants.
Combine 20 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil.
Steep the mixture for 2 hours under room temperature allowing the herbal extract to infuse into the oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Mix the infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax and heat until melted then pour into containers cooling and hardening completely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange creams helps with period cramps because they contain a unique combination of herbs that work together to relax uterine muscles, reduce inflammation, and improve circulation. The bitter orange peel extract, in particular, has been shown to have a profound effect on soothing menstrual cramp pain by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions. remedy_properties: Vasodilation: Bitter orange cream's vasodilatory properties help to relax blood vessels, which can reduce menstrual cramp pain and discomfort by increasing blood flow to the affected areas.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal bitter orange creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and limonoids that can help reduce inflammation in the uterus, which may contribute to period cramps.

Anxiolytic and sedative effects: The sedative properties of bitter orange cream's active compounds, such as citral and citronellal, can help alleviate anxiety and stress associated with menstrual cramps, promoting relaxation and reducing overall discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing menstrual cramp pain.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and terpenes, barks help to relax muscles, reducing spasms and cramping associated with menstruation.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 200 grams of dried citrus x aurantium peel, crush it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered citrus x aurantium with 100 milliliters of jojoba oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax has melted and the mixture is smooth.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to around 60 degrees celsius, then whip it with a hand mixer until it thickens into a creamy consistency.
Pour the citrus aurantium cream into sterilized glass jars, label them, and store them in the refrigerator for up to two months.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for period cramps are topical ointments infused with natural ingredients that help alleviate menstrual pain and discomfort. They work by reducing inflammation, relaxing muscles, and promoting hormone balance. The best herbal creams for period cramps include Glycyrrhiza glabra, Zingiber officinale, Cimicifuga racemosa, Angelica archangelica, Lavandula angustifolia, Vitex agnus-castus, Ginkgo biloba, Paeonia lactiflora, Viburnum opulus, and Trifolium pratense. If left untreated, period cramps can significantly impact daily life, causing missed work or school, disrupted sleep, and decreased productivity.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to period cramps that are treated with herbal creams are menstrual migraines, fibromyalgia, and endometriosis. These herbal creams often contain soothing ingredients like lavender, chamomile, and arnica, which help to reduce inflammation and relax muscles. They may also include warming agents such as capsaicin or ginger to increase blood flow and relieve pain. By targeting these underlying causes of discomfort, herbal creams can provide effective relief from cramp-like symptoms without harsh chemicals or prescription medications.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with period cramps is a blend of arnica, ginger, and clary sage. Arnica cream reduces inflammation and eases pain, while ginger cream warms the abdomen and soothes cramps. Clary sage cream calms the mind and body, reducing stress and promoting relaxation. Applying a mixture of these creams to the lower abdomen can provide quick relief from period cramps. They can be used individually or in combination for maximum effect.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Complementary therapies in medicine, primary dysmenorrhoea is a common complaint experienced by many females in their reproductive years. The study, which assessed the efficacy of medicinal plants in treating primary dysmenorrhoea, involved a thorough database search and the evaluation of 22 randomized controlled trials (RCTs) published between 2008 and 2016. The results of the study showed that most of the evaluated medicinal plants showed evidence of efficacy in relieving menstrual pain in at least one RCT, indicating that herbal creams may be a beneficial treatment option for period cramps.
